questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Colligative properties are very important properties of the solution. These properties are based on amount of solute and solvent only. They do not depend upon the identity of the particles. The vapour pressures of pure ethanol and pure methanol are 44 mmHg and 88 mmHg at a certain temperature $\mathrm{T}(\mathrm{K})$. They form an ideal solutionParagraph: Choose the incorrect option among following
options: Above graph correctly represents the variation of vapour pressure with mole fraction for an ideal solution
If a non-volatile solute is added to pure methanol then only line AD appears in above graph
At point $F, \frac{P_M^o}{P_E^o}=\frac{n_E}{n_M}$ ( $n$ represents moles in solution)
If mole fraction of each is 0.5 , then vapor pressure of solution is 0.087 atm
questions.solution:
At point $F$
$\begin{aligned}
& P_M=P_E \\
& P_M^{\circ} X_M=P_E^{\circ} X_E \\
& \frac{P_M^{\circ} n_M}{\left(n_M+n_E\right)}=\frac{P_E^{\circ} n_E}{\left(n_M+n_E\right)} \\
& \frac{P_M^{\circ}}{P_E^{\circ}}=\frac{n_E}{n_M}
\end{aligned}$

Total pressure at 0.5 mole fraction of each
$P_T=(44 \times 0.5)+(88 \times 0.5)$
$\begin{aligned} & =66 \mathrm{~mm} \mathrm{Hg} \\ & =\frac{66}{760}=0.087 \mathrm{~atm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Osmosis is a process of movement of solvent particles from a region of lower to higher concentration through semi permeable membrane. The pressure exerted by the liquids on semi permeable membrane at the time of equilibrium is called osmotic pressure. If the pressure more than osmotic pressure is applied at the more concentration side, reverse osmosis takes place, which is used for the purification of water
Question: Select the correct statement for the above diagram
options: If semi permeable membrane is permeable to only $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$, level of liquid in the left arm will decrease
If membrane is permeable to $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$and $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$all, the level of liquid in two arms is different
In the reverse osmosis process water will move from NaCl side to $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ side
all of these
questions.solution: In reverse osmosis water moves from more concentrated to less concentrated solution
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Colligative properties are very important properties of the solution. These properties are based on amount of solute and solvent only. They do not depend upon the identity of the particles. The vapour pressures of pure ethanol and pure methanol are 44 mmHg and 88 mmHg at a certain temperature $T(K)$. They form an ideal solutionParagraph: Select the correct statement.
options: Vapour pressure is a colligative property
Fluid inside the blood cell is isotonic with $0.9 \% \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{V} \mathrm{NaCl}$ solution
Ebullioscopic or cryoscopic constants are properties of solution only
Spreading NaCl on the ice deposited on road will cause increase of freezing point
questions.solution: $k_b$ or $k_f$ are the properties of solvent only.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Osmosis is a process of movement of solvent particles from a region of lower to higher concentration through semi permeable membrane. The pressure exerted by the liquids on semi permeable membrane at the time of equilibrium is called osmotic pressure. If the pressure more than osmotic pressure is applied at the more concentration side, reverse osmosis takes place, which is used for the purification of water
Question: A water desalination plant is set up near a salt marsh containing water that is 0.1 NaCl . The minimum pressure $(\mathrm{P})$ that must be applied at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to purify the water by reverse osmosis is
options: $P \geq 4.926$ atm
$\mathrm{P} \geq 2.46 \mathrm{~atm}$
$P \leq 2.46$ atm
$P \leq 4.926$ atm
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Concentration of a solution can be represented in many ways. Some of the important concentration terms are as follows : $\text { Molarity of solution }=\frac{\text { number of moles }}{\text { volume of solution in litre }}$ Let a solution is prepared by dissolving w gm of solute of $\mathrm{mol} . \mathrm{wt}$. M in V ml water. $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \text { Number of moles of solute dissolved }=\frac{w}{M} \\ & \text { molality }=\frac{\text { number of moles of solute }}{\text { mass of solvent in gram }} \times 1000 \\ & \% \mathrm{w} / \mathrm{v}=\frac{\text { mass of solute in } \mathrm{gm}}{\text { mass of solution in } \mathrm{ml}} \times 100 \end{aligned}$Question: Equal volume of $1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NaOH}, 10 \% \mathrm{w} / \mathrm{v} \mathrm{NaOH}$ and $1 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{d}=1.2 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{ml})$ are mixed then what will be M of final solution. (All aqueous)
options: None of these
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Concentration of a solution can be represented in many ways. Some of the important concentration terms are as follows : $\text { Molarity of solution }=\frac{\text { number of moles }}{\text { volume of solution in litre }}$ Let a solution is prepared by dissolving w gm of solute of $\mathrm{mol} . \mathrm{wt}$. M in V ml water. $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \text { Number of moles of solute dissolved }=\frac{w}{M} \\ & \text { molality }=\frac{\text { number of moles of solute }}{\text { mass of solvent in gram }} \times 1000 \\ & \% \mathrm{w} / \mathrm{v}=\frac{\text { mass of solute in } \mathrm{gm}}{\text { mass of solution in } \mathrm{ml}} \times 100 \end{aligned}$Question: 120 gm of 1 M aqueous NaOH solution ( $\mathrm{d}=1.2 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{ml}$ ) is mixed with x ml of $9.8 \% \mathrm{w} / \mathrm{v} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution. pH of resulting solution is 13 then value of $x$ will be :
